response:
# Perspective Audit — Address Autocomplete With Fast Timeout and Double-Action Enter ## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed Deep review runs for the perspectives the a11y-critic flagged at MEDIUM or HIGH: - **Keyboard & Motor Access** — HIGH - **Screen Reader & Semantic Structure** — MEDIUM - **Cognitive & Neurodivergent** — HIGH Not reviewed (rated LOW / not escalated): Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access. Contrast observations that surfaced incidentally (the `#bbdefb` highlighted-option state ≈1.2:1 on white; `#888` no-results text ≈3.5:1) belong to **Environmental Contrast (1.4.11 / 1.4.3)**, which is LOW — I did **not** run that checklist and do not raise those as formal findings. They are noted only where they compound a keyboard/SR barrier. --- ## Perspective 5 — Keyboard & Motor Access (HIGH) **JTBD checklist results** | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---| | All interactive elements reachable/operable by keyboard (2.1.1) | **FINDING** (behavioral) | Input + submit reach via Tab; options selectable via Arrow+Enter — **but** the 800 ms auto-dismiss (fixture L51–59) makes options transiently unreachable, and the only keyboard selection path (Enter) force-submits the form (L70–78). Structurally reachable, behaviorally compromised. | | No keyboard traps (2.1.2) | **PASS** | No focus containment; Tab/Shift+Tab move freely. | | Skip link targets main (2.4.1) | **N/A** | Page-level concern; component fragment. | | Focus order logical (2.4.3) | **PASS** | input → submit; options correctly excluded from Tab order (APG combobox convention). | | Focus indicator ≥2px, ≥3:1 (2.4.7 / 2.4.13) | **PASS** | Input `outline: 3px solid #005fcc; outline-offset: 2px` (CSS L172–175); submit `:focus-visible` outline (L241–244). 2.4.13 is AAA — not scored as a barrier. | | Multi-point gestures have single-pointer alt (2.5.1) | **N/A** | No gestures. | | Activation on up-event (2.5.2) | **PASS** | Native `onClick` fires on pointerup/click. | | Visible label matches accessible name (2.5.3) | **PASS** | ``; button text = accessible name. | | Drag has click alternative (2.5.7) | **N/A** | No drag operations. | | Targets ≥24×24 CSS px (2.5.8) | **PASS** | Option rows ≈39px tall (padding 10px + ~19px text), input ≈40px, button ≈44px. | | Portrait & landscape (1.3.4) | **PASS** | No orientation lock; `max-width` layout. | The **structural** keyboard mechanics are clean — no trap, sane focus order, visible indicators, adequate target sizes, up-event activation. The HIGH alarm is justified by two **behavioral** defects whose WCAG homes sit in the Cognitive perspective but which land hardest on keyboard/motor users; they are documented in full under Perspective 7 and cross-referenced here. One net-new keyboard finding follows. **Finding:** No `Escape` handler to dismiss the open suggestion list. The WAI-ARIA APG combobox pattern expects Escape to close the popup (and/or clear); here the list only closes via blur, re-typing, or the 800 ms timer. A keyboard user who opens the list and decides to keep their typed value has no direct dismiss key. **WCAG:** 2.1.1 Keyboard (APG Combobox interaction expectation) **Perspective:** Keyboard & Motor Access **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** `handleKeyDown` (fixture L61–79) handles only ArrowDown/ArrowUp/Enter; no `Escape` branch. **Fix:** Add `if (e.key === 'Escape') { setShowSuggestions(false); }` (leaving the typed query intact), consistent with the APG "Editable Combobox with List Autocomplete" pattern. **Cross-referenced keyboard/motor impact (owned under Perspective 7):** - **800 ms auto-dismiss (2.2.1)** — a switch user, a user with tremor, or anyone slow to press the first Arrow after the list appears loses it before acting. The timer only resets on `selectedIdx`/`showSuggestions` change, so *reading* or *reaching for the mouse* does not keep it alive. See CN-1. - **Enter double-action (3.2.2)** — the sole keyboard selection key also fires `formRef.current.submit()`, so a keyboard user cannot pick a suggestion *without* being navigated to payment. See CN-2. --- ## Perspective 6 — Screen Reader & Semantic Structure (MEDIUM) **JTBD checklist results** | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---| | Landmark regions present/correct (1.3.1) | **N/A** | Component fragment; page landmarks not in scope. `
` present but unnamed (not required). | | Heading hierarchy logical (2.4.6) | **N/A (cannot assess)** | `

Shipping Address

` is reasonable in-section; full outline is page-level. | | Informative images have alt; decorative `alt=""` (1.1.1) | **PASS** | No ``; spinner is a `
`. | | DOM order matches visual order (1.3.2) | **PASS** | Source order = visual order. | | Unique descriptive `` (2.4.2) | **N/A** | Page-level. | | Link text describes destination (2.4.4) | **N/A** | No links. | | `lang` on `<html>` (3.1.1) / foreign phrases (3.1.2) | **N/A** | Page-level. | | Every input has associated visible label (3.3.2) | **PASS** | `<label htmlFor="address-input">` + `aria-describedby="address-hint"` correctly wired (fixture L91, 99, 102). Genuinely good. | | Errors identify field, associated via `aria-describedby` (3.3.1) | **N/A** | No validation/error state exists to evaluate. | | Custom widgets use correct ARIA roles (4.1.2) | **FINDING — RED FLAG** | Combobox/listbox built from bare `<div>`s: no `role="combobox"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `aria-autocomplete`, `aria-activedescendant`; no `role="listbox"` (L109) or `role="option"`/`aria-selected` (L112). | | Status messages use `aria-live` (4.1.3) | **FINDING** | Loading spinner (L105), "No matching addresses" (L123–126), and results-appeared state are all silent. | | SPA route changes announced | **N/A** | No routing in component. | **Finding [SR-1]:** The autocomplete is a custom combobox-with-listbox widget implemented entirely from non-semantic `<div>`s with zero ARIA. A screen reader user is never told suggestions exist, cannot perceive how many, cannot hear the active option as Arrow keys move the visual highlight (`selectedIdx` drives only a CSS class), and gets no `aria-expanded` open/closed state. The widget is effectively absent from the accessibility tree while being visually central. **WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** CRITICAL (auto — fires the perspective red flag "Custom widget with no ARIA role") **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design — owns the APG pattern selection) **Evidence:** Input `<input ...>` has no combobox ARIA (fixture L92–101); suggestions container `<div className="suggestions-list">` has no role (L108–109); items `<div className="suggestion-item ...">` have no role/`aria-selected` (L111–116). Active option conveyed only via `.highlighted` class (CSS L217). **Fix:** Implement the WAI-ARIA APG *Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete* pattern: `role="combobox"` + `aria-expanded` + `aria-controls` + `aria-autocomplete="list"` on the input; `role="listbox"` on the container with a stable `id`; `role="option"` + unique `id` + `aria-selected` on each item; set `aria-activedescendant` on the input to the active option's `id` during Arrow navigation. **Finding [SR-2]:** The "No matching addresses found" message is a status update rendered in a plain `<p>` with no live region, so a screen reader user who types a non-matching string receives **no feedback at all** — no results, no error, silence. They cannot distinguish "system still working" from "nothing matched." **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author — message wording) **Evidence:** `<p className="no-results">No matching addresses found.</p>` (fixture L123–126); no `role="status"`/`aria-live`. **Fix:** Wrap the message in `role="status"` (or `aria-live="polite"`). When results are present, also expose an accessible count (e.g., a visually-hidden `role="status"` "6 suggestions available") so results-available is announced too. **Finding [SR-3]:** The loading spinner is CSS-only with no accessible status — no `aria-busy` on the combobox, no text, no `role="status"`. A screen reader user gets no "loading/searching" cue. **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages **Perspective:** Screen Reader & Semantic Structure **Severity:** MINOR (the mock debounce is ~200 ms so the state is very transient; **this rises to MAJOR against any real backend latency**, where a silent multi-hundred-ms fetch is a genuine gap) **Route to:** Front-End Dev **Evidence:** `{loading && <div className="autocomplete-spinner" />}` (fixture L105); `.autocomplete-spinner` is decorative CSS (CSS L183–194). **Fix:** Set `aria-busy="true"` on the combobox while `loading`, and/or render a visually-hidden `role="status"` "Searching addresses…". **MEDIUM→ effective severity note:** the critic's MEDIUM alarm under-states what the deep pass found. The red-flag `4.1.2` failure makes the widget's real screen-reader severity **CRITICAL**. The alarm correctly triggered the audit; the audit sets the severity. --- ## Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent (HIGH) **JTBD checklist results** | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---| | Error messages suggest specific correction (3.3.3) | **N/A** | No validation/error handling present. | | Multi-step form doesn't re-ask earlier data (3.3.7) | **N/A** | Single step. | | Accessible auth; paste/autofill work (3.3.8) | **N/A** | No authentication. Paste is **not** blocked. | | Navigation same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | **N/A** | Single component. | | Same-function components consistent (3.2.4) | **N/A** | Single component. | | Help mechanism consistent location (3.2.6) | **N/A** | Single component. | | Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | **PASS** | Focusing the input triggers nothing; blur only hides the list. | | Changing a control value doesn't auto-submit/navigate (3.2.2) | **FINDING** | Enter on a highlighted option calls `formRef.current.submit()` — selection *and* form submission in one keystroke (fixture L70–78). | | Destructive actions have undo/confirm/review (3.3.4) | **PARTIAL / see CN-2** | "Continue to payment" is a forward step, not destructive; but Enter removes the chance to *review* the selected address before proceeding. | | More than one way to reach a page (2.4.5) | **N/A** | Single component. | | Personal-data fields have appropriate `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | **FINDING** | `autoComplete="off"` on a street-address field (fixture L100). | | Timeout warnings give ≥20s to extend (2.2.1) | **FINDING** | 800 ms auto-dismiss with no warning, extend, or disable (fixture L51–59). | | Content avoids unexplained jargon / complex sentences | **PASS** | Labels and hint are plain ("Start typing to see address suggestions"). | **Neurodivergent-specific checks** | Check | Result | Evidence | |---|---|---| | Predictable layout, no unexpected shifts | **FINDING (compounds CN-1)** | Suggestions appear then vanish on a hidden timer with no user action — unpredictable content change. | | Clear visual hierarchy | **PASS** | Heading, label, hint, spaced controls. | | No autoplay media / unprompted modals | **PASS** | None. | | Instructions don't rely on memory of prior steps | **PASS** | Hint is persistent. | **Finding [CN-1]:** Suggestions self-dismiss 800 ms after the list appears (or after the last Arrow keypress), with no warning, no way to extend, and no way to turn it off. The timer resets only on `selectedIdx`/`showSuggestions` change — so *reading* the list, *deciding*, or *reaching for the pointer* does not keep it alive. Users who read slowly, use a screen magnifier (panning across the viewport eats the budget), have a motor impairment, or use a screen reader lose the suggestions before they can act. **WCAG:** 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable (Level A) **Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent (impact spans Keyboard & Motor) **Severity:** **MAJOR** — *calibrated on impact, not checklist reflex.* The suggestion **feature** is effectively unusable for the affected populations, but the underlying input remains fully labeled and operable, so the core task (type the address manually, then "Continue") is still achievable. This is degradation/loss of an aid, not a total task block → MAJOR. **It becomes CRITICAL** if a suggestion is ever the only path to a valid/normalized value (e.g., server-side address validation that rejects free-typed input). No red-flag auto-CRITICAL rule fires for this in the Cognitive checklist, which is consistent with the MAJOR rating. **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) — the existence and duration of the timer is a UX/behavior decision; removal/extension is the FE change. **Evidence:** Second `useEffect` (fixture L51–59): `timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(() => setShowSuggestions(false), 800)` on deps `[showSuggestions, selectedIdx]`. **Fix:** Remove the auto-dismiss entirely (dropdowns should persist until blur, Escape, selection, or query change). If an idle-hide is genuinely required, make it user-adjustable and ≥20× longer, or gate it so it never fires while the field has focus. **Finding [CN-2]:** Pressing Enter on a highlighted suggestion both fills the field *and* submits the form via native `formRef.current.submit()`, navigating to payment. Two problems: (a) an unexpected change of context from a selection keystroke — the user's model is "Enter picks the suggestion," not "Enter checks out"; they never get to review the chosen address; (b) `formRef.current.submit()` bypasses the React `onSubmit` handler's `preventDefault`, triggering a real page submission/navigation and discarding SPA state. A keyboard user cannot select a suggestion *without* triggering this. **WCAG:** 3.2.2 On Input (Level A) **Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent (impact spans Keyboard & Motor) **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) — the native-submit call is an implementation defect; intended Enter behavior is a UX decision. **Evidence:** `handleKeyDown` Enter branch (fixture L70–78): `setQuery(...); setShowSuggestions(false); formRef.current.submit();`. **Fix:** On Enter, select only — set the value, close the list, keep focus in the field. Remove `formRef.current.submit()`. Let the user review the filled address and activate "Continue to payment" deliberately (or press Enter a second time on the now-empty combobox to submit, per APG). **Finding [CN-3]:** `autoComplete="off"` on a street-address field disables browser autofill and prevents the input's purpose from being programmatically determined. Users with memory or motor challenges lose the ability to autofill their saved address and must type it in full. **WCAG:** 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose (Level AA) **Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR — a substantive barrier for the perspective's users; a manual-typing workaround exists, but 1.3.5 exists precisely because manual entry *is* the barrier for this group. (Likely set to stop the native autofill UI colliding with the custom dropdown — a common but harmful trade-off.) **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** `autoComplete="off"` on the input (fixture L100). **Fix:** Use a valid token — `autoComplete="street-address"` (or `"address-line1"`) — and coordinate the native and custom popups (e.g., `aria-autocomplete="list"` on the combobox) rather than suppressing autofill outright. --- ## Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** AddressAutocomplete (address input with fast-timeout suggestions + double-action Enter) **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard & Motor (HIGH), Screen Reader & Semantic (MEDIUM), Cognitive & Neurodivergent (HIGH) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 1 | | MAJOR | 4 | | MINOR | 2 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | ### Perspectives That Passed None of the three escalated perspectives passed cleanly — each surfaced at least one MAJOR-or-higher finding. Genuine strengths worth preserving: correct `<label>`/`for` + `aria-describedby` wiring, visible ≥3px focus indicators on both focusable controls, options correctly excluded from the Tab order, adequate target sizes (≥24px), no keyboard trap, and up-event activation. ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - Custom combobox/listbox built from bare `<div>`s, zero ARIA — Screen Reader — **CRITICAL** — 4.1.2 - "No results" message not announced (no live region) — Screen Reader — MAJOR — 4.1.3 - Enter selects *and* submits via native `form.submit()` — Cognitive — MAJOR — 3.2.2 - `autocomplete="off"` on a street-address field — Cognitive — MAJOR — 1.3.5 - Loading spinner has no accessible status — Screen Reader — MINOR — 4.1.3 - No `Escape` to dismiss the open list — Keyboard & Motor — MINOR — 2.1.1 (APG) **UX Design** - 800 ms auto-dismiss of suggestions, no warning/extend/disable — Cognitive (spans Keyboard & Motor) — MAJOR — 2.2.1 **Visual Design** - None raised. (The `#bbdefb` active-option and `#888` no-results contrast weaknesses sit under Environmental Contrast 1.4.11/1.4.3, which was LOW and not audited — flagged here only as compounding context, not as scored findings.) **Content Author** - None as primary owner. Secondary on the "No results" wording (SR-2). ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification None. All findings are code-level and confirmable from source (file:line cited); no content-level items (captions, transcripts, alt-text quality, reading level) are in scope for this component. ### Recommended Next Step **BLOCK.** One CRITICAL finding (4.1.2 — the interactive suggestion widget is absent from the accessibility tree, an auto red flag) makes the component non-shippable for screen reader users as built. Before ship, minimum bar: (1) implement the APG combobox/listbox ARIA (SR-1); (2) remove the native `form.submit()` from the Enter path (CN-2); (3) remove or make-adjustable the 800 ms auto-dismiss (CN-1). The two MAJOR content/status findings (SR-2, CN-3) and the MINORs should be resolved in the same pass, then re-run **a11y-test** (axe-core + real-keyboard Playwright) to verify Arrow/Enter/Escape behavior and live-region announcements. --- ## A11y Evidence Findings (contract-compatible blocks for CRITICAL/MAJOR) ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: autocomplete-combobox-no-aria fingerprint: 4f1a2c9e7b3d5086 source: perspective-audit Screen Reader & Semantic Structure checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA APG Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A-AA; 4.1.2 is in scope severity: CRITICAL perspective_alarms: screen_reader_semantic=HIGH (deep review; incoming critic alarm MEDIUM), keyboard_motor=MEDIUM evidence: input has no combobox ARIA (fixture L92–101); `<div className="suggestions-list">` no role (L108–109); `<div className="suggestion-item">` no role/aria-selected (L111–116); active option conveyed only via `.highlighted` CSS class (CSS L217) reproduction_steps: source review only — with a screen reader, focus the input, type "Main", press ArrowDown; no combobox/listbox/option roles, no aria-expanded, no aria-activedescendant are announced expected_behavior: screen reader announces combobox, expanded state, option count, and the active option as Arrow keys move through the list actual_behavior: suggestions and their active state are absent from the accessibility tree; the widget is silent to assistive technology trend: new ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: autocomplete-800ms-auto-dismiss fingerprint: b7c04e19a2f6d3c8 source: perspective-audit Cognitive & Neurodivergent checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable (Level A) section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A-AA; 2.2.1 is in scope severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: cognitive_neurodivergent=HIGH, keyboard_motor=HIGH evidence: second useEffect sets `setTimeout(() => setShowSuggestions(false), 800)` on deps `[showSuggestions, selectedIdx]` (fixture L51–59); no warning, extend, or disable control reproduction_steps: source review only — type an address, stop, wait 800 ms without pressing Arrow; the suggestion list disappears expected_behavior: suggestions persist until blur, Escape, selection, or query change; any time limit is adjustable/extendable/disable-able per 2.2.1 actual_behavior: list auto-hides after 800 ms of inactivity, unusable for users who need more time; escalates to CRITICAL if a suggestion is the only path to a valid value trend: new ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: autocomplete-enter-double-action fingerprint: 9d3e5a71c8042bf6 source: perspective-audit Cognitive & Neurodivergent checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 3.2.2 On Input (Level A) section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A-AA; 3.2.2 is in scope severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: cognitive_neurodivergent=HIGH, keyboard_motor=HIGH evidence: Enter branch of handleKeyDown runs `setQuery(...); setShowSuggestions(false); formRef.current.submit();` (fixture L70–78), bypassing the React onSubmit preventDefault reproduction_steps: source review only — type an address, ArrowDown to a suggestion, press Enter; the form submits and navigates to payment in the same keystroke expected_behavior: Enter selects the suggestion and keeps focus in the field for review; submission is a separate, deliberate action actual_behavior: selection and full-page form submission fire together; the user cannot pick a suggestion without being navigated away, and SPA state is discarded trend: new ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: autocomplete-off-on-address fingerprint: 2a8f6013e4b7c9d1 source: perspective-audit Cognitive & Neurodivergent checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose (Level AA) section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A-AA; 1.3.5 is a WCAG 2.1 addition — verify target conformance baseline severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: cognitive_neurodivergent=HIGH evidence: `autoComplete="off"` on the street-address input (fixture L100) reproduction_steps: source review only — focus the field with saved autofill data available; the browser offers no address autofill and the field's purpose is not programmatically exposed expected_behavior: field uses a valid token (e.g., `autocomplete="street-address"`) so autofill works and purpose is determinable actual_behavior: autofill disabled and purpose hidden, forcing full manual entry for users who rely on autofill trend: new ### A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: autocomplete-no-results-not-announced fingerprint: c1e97b42a05d8f36 source: perspective-audit Screen Reader & Semantic Structure checklist wcag_or_apg: WCAG 2.2 4.1.3 Status Messages (Level AA) section_508_fpc_context: Revised Section 508 incorporates WCAG 2.0 A-AA; 4.1.3 is a WCAG 2.1 addition — verify target conformance baseline severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: screen_reader_semantic=HIGH (deep review; incoming critic alarm MEDIUM) evidence: `<p className="no-results">No matching addresses found.</p>` rendered without a live region (fixture L123–126) reproduction_steps: source review only — with a screen reader, type a string that matches nothing; no status is announced expected_behavior: "No matching addresses found" is announced via role="status"/aria-live="polite"; results-available count is likewise exposed actual_behavior: the no-results state is silent to assistive technology, leaving the user without feedback trend: new

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
